concept: Fossils - time turning soft tissue into stone
quote: Time destroys everything that is temporary. It preserves everything that is true.

visual_prompt.primary_prompt: Infinite slow camera descent through polished obsidian rock faces (#0C0C0C to #1A1A1A). The walls are mirror-smooth - dark volcanic glass that reflects distorted echoes of the figure beside it. Embedded in the obsidian: human handprints and silhouettes crystallized in gold (#C6A94F), perfectly preserved, glowing with quiet authority. Crimson mineral veins (#DC143C) pulse through the rock in slow rhythmic waves. Bone-white light (#F5F2EB) enters from a single overhead source - ancient and absolute. The obsidian reflects the figure back at slightly different angles - multiple truths of the same person preserved in the same stone. No text. Vertical 9:16. Center frame clear for subtitles. Mood: confrontational stillness, geological permanence, truth made physical.

script.full_text: They buried you and thought it was the end. They did not realize they were just providing the pressure required for you to become permanent. Every layer added was weight. Every year of silence was compression. What they called burial was preparation. Soft things rot. Surface things fade. But when you are crushed by the weight of expectations, what is false burns away. Only your core remains. Time does not destroy what is real. It removes everything that is not. Let the world be heavy. It is only making your mark impossible to erase.

segments.text: They buried you and thought it was the end. They did not realize they were just providing the pressure required for you to become permanent.
segments.visual_prompt: Opening frame: a pair of hands pressing into dark wet obsidian clay (#1A1A1A). The impression is immediate and certain - bone-white light (#F5F2EB) outlines the fingers from above. Dark sand begins to fold over the hands slowly. The surface becomes a mirror reflecting the hands from below. Gold light (#C6A94F) pulses once from the center of the impression. Camera holds close as the first layer seals. What looks like an ending is the start of a very long preservation. Mood: quiet inevitability, no struggle.

segments.text: Every layer added was weight. Every year of silence was compression. What they called burial was preparation. Soft things rot. Surface things fade. But when you are crushed by the weight of expectations, what is false burns away. Only your core remains.
segments.visual_prompt: Time compression through obsidian strata. Mirror-smooth layers (#0C0C0C, #1A1A1A, #242424) accumulate in geological waves. The buried impression is visible through each translucent dark layer - growing brighter with each stratum, not dimmer. Crimson veins (#DC143C) thread through the compression like a second circulatory system still running. Bone-white crystalline deposits (#F5F2EB) lock into the impression from the outside in, hardening it into something permanent. The soft impression is becoming obsidian itself. Camera drifts along the mirror face - unhurried, geological. Mood: patient weight, weight becoming truth.

segments.text: Time does not destroy what is real. It removes everything that is not. Let the world be heavy. It is only making your mark impossible to erase.
segments.visual_prompt: The camera arrives at the fully crystallized fossil - a luminous gold handprint (#C6A94F) set in deep obsidian (#0C0C0C), glowing from within. The obsidian wall reflects it back perfectly. Two identical truths - the fossil and its reflection - both permanent, both undeniable. Camera holds still. Crimson veins (#DC143C) pulse once through the surrounding rock then go quiet. Bone-white light (#F5F2EB) holds steady from above. The reflection does not waver. Final frame: the gold fossil and its reflection face each other across the dark mirror surface. Impossible to erase. Mood: absolute stillness, complete.

instagram_config.caption:
Become permanent.

Pressure does not just break. It preserves what matters.

Save this for the days the weight feels like the end.
